phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The Story is persisted and `agent::ready`.

## Summary

Ran the `/plan` pipeline headlessly for the change-password capability. One cohesive Story authored (default-single policy — the endpoint, its store support, and its tests are one capability slice), passing all deterministic gates.

**Story #3** — *Add POST /password so signed-in users can change their password* (`agent::ready`)
- https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-story-scope-mandrel-17870226/issues/3
- Cohort label: `plan-run::2be4813b`

**Contract captured in the Spec**, grounded in the existing code:
- **Auth**: Bearer token via the existing `authenticate()` helper; 401 before body parsing on missing/invalid token.
- **Body**: reuses `parseJsonObject` (malformed/non-object → 400) and `isNonBlankString` (missing/blank/non-string `newPassword` → 400), matching `/signup` and `/login`.
- **Success**: fresh `hashPassword(newPassword)` salt+hash persisted; **all** of the user's sessions deleted (including the caller's own token) → 200, password/hash never echoed.
- **Security invariants** as acceptance criteria: pre-change token → 401 afterward; old password → 401 on login; new password → 200 with a working token; `405` on wrong method.
- New store methods flagged: `updateUserPassword(userId, salt, hash)` and `deleteSessionsByUser(userId)`.

**Verify**: `node --test test/api.test.js` (e2e) + `npm run lint` (validate).

**Pipeline notes**: Gate #1 and Gate #2 auto-proceeded under `--yes`. Both plan critics returned `dispatch: false` (skips ledgered), text-hygiene clean. Dry-run passed all gates before the live persist. As instructed, this session ran **planning only** — no delivery was started.

Story #3 is `landed`. Delivery is complete. Relaying the terminal envelope:

```json
{"kind":"story-deliver-terminal","storyId":3,"status":"landed","phase":"post-land","storyBranch":"story-3","baseBranch":"main","pr":{"number":4,"url":"https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-story-scope-mandrel-17870226/pull/4","autoMergeEnabled":true,"state":"MERGED","checksStatus":"success"},"gates":{"validation":"passed","baseSync":"passed","codeReview":"passed"},"tail":{"followUps":true,"statusResync":true,"refCleanup":true,"baseFastForward":false},"blocked":null,"failure":null,"nextCommand":null,"elapsedSeconds":15}
```

## Delivery complete

**Story #3 — Add POST /password — `landed`** via [PR #4](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-story-scope-mandrel-17870226/pull/4) (squash-merged to `main`, `agent::done`).

The task named an "Epic," but the planning run authored a single Story-only ticket (v2 has no Epic ticket type). I discovered it (#3, the only open ticket, `plan-run::2be4813b`) and delivered the existing plan unchanged — no re-planning.

**What shipped:**
- `src/server.js` — `POST /password` handler: authenticates (401 *before* body parse), validates `newPassword` via the existing `isNonBlankString` / `parseJsonObject` rules (400 on bad input), re-hashes with a fresh scrypt salt, and returns `{ "ok": true }` — never echoing the password or hash. `/password` added to the 405 known-path list.
- `src/store.js` — `updateUserPassword`, `deleteSessionsByUser`, and a `transaction` helper so the hash update and session wipe commit or roll back together (no half-updated account).
- `test/api.test.js` — six new e2e tests (PW AC-1…AC-6) covering the security invariants: old token revoked, old password rejected, new password works.

**Security invariants verified** — all sessions (including the caller's) are revoked on change, so pre-change tokens 401, old-password login 401, new-password login 200 with a working token.

**Gates:** 16/16 tests pass · lint clean · close-validation / base-sync / code-review all passed (1 non-blocking suggestion, 0 critical/high/medium). Worktree reaped; lease released.

One cosmetic note from the tail: the post-land `main` fast-forward was skipped because the primary checkout's working tree is dirty (the pre-existing untracked `temp/`). The merge stands — this only means your local `main` ref wasn't fast-forwarded; `git fetch`/`pull` will sync it.
